questions.text: Which description best matches Threats in CCNA Objective 5.0 Security Fundamentals?

answers.text: Threats are potential events or actors that can exploit weaknesses and harm systems, data, or operations.
Vulnerabilities are weaknesses in systems, configurations, software, processes, or controls that can be exploited.
Exploits are methods or code that take advantage of vulnerabilities to cause unauthorized behavior.
Mitigation techniques reduce the likelihood or impact of threats exploiting vulnerabilities.
